data.Modification: proline oxidation to pyroglutamic acid/Tryptophan oxidation to oxolactone/aldehyde and ketone modifications
Removal of initiator methionine from protein N-terminus, then acetylation of the new N-terminus
Iodoacetamide derivative/Addition of Glycine/Addition of G
Tyrosine oxidation to 2-aminotyrosine/Conversion of carboxylic acid to hydroxamic acid
Removal of initiator methionine from protein N-terminus/Deletion of M
Loss of arginine due to transpeptidation/Deletion of R
Loss of C-terminal K from Heavy Chain of MAb/Deletion of K

project.title: Direct visualization of ERAD nanocompartments within the cell
project.projectDescription: ER-associated degradation (ERAD) is an evolutionarily-conserved quality control mechanism where misfolded proteins are removed from the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) and degraded by the ubiquitin-proteasome system. While many ERAD-mediating proteins have been identified, it is unknown how they are spatially organized within the cell. Using in situ cryo-electron tomography to image the native molecular landscape of Chlamydomonas, we discovered that ERAD proteins are concentrated within ~200 nm cytosolic foci that contact the ER membrane away from the ER-Golgi interface. These ribosome-excluding ERAD nanocompartments consist of a core of clustered proteasomes surrounded by Cdc48. Active proteasomes directly engage with the ER membrane, indicating an additional Cdc48-independent mechanism for extraction of misfolded proteins. Our study reveals that ERAD has precise cellular architecture, which likely enables efficient protein quality control.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Proteins were digested by in-solution digestion protocol and peptides were fractionated to 3 SCX fractions via StageTip prior to LCMSMS analysis using Q Exactive HF mass spectrometer
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Raw data were processed using MaxQuant computational proteomics suite. Proteins and peptides were filtered at 1% FDR.

references.referenceLine: Albert S, Wietrzynski W, Lee CW, Schaffer M, Beck F, Schuller JM, Salomé PA, Plitzko JM, Baumeister W, Engel BD. Direct visualization of degradation microcompartments at the ER membrane. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A. 2020 117(2):1069-1080

Characteristics[CleavageAgent]: digested overnight with 4 µg of trypsin.
Comment[FractionationMethod]: purification and fractionation in a SCX StageTip.
Comment[NumberOfFractions]: All three fractions from the purification were loaded into a 15-cm-long and 75-µm-wide column (New Objective)
Comment[Separation]: loaded into a 15-cm-long and 75-µm-wide column (New Objective), packed with 1.9-µm C18 reprosil beads (Dr Maisch GmbH).
Comment[GradientTime]: The peptides were eluted over a gradient for ∼140 min
Comment[Instrument]: bench top orbitrap mass spectrometer (Q Exactive HF; Thermo Fisher)
Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance]: initial parent ion mass deviation of 6 ppm
